summary: Tested two bugs: Bug 1 (platform admin tenant name update syncs app_settings.store_name) and Bug 2 (partner admin single save updates TopNav). Backend Bug 1 is FIXED (10/10 backend tests pass). Frontend Bug 1 is FIXED (TenantsTab shows updated store_name and partner Organization Info reflects platform admin's name change). Frontend Bug 2 is NOT FIXED — TopNav still shows stale 'website_settings.store_name' ("Rookie") instead of the saved value after ONE save from Organization Info.

ui_bugs.issue: BUG 2 NOT FIXED: After partner admin saves Store Name in Organization Info, TopNav shows stale 'website_settings.store_name' value ('Rookie') instead of the newly saved value ('BugFixTest 4935'). The refreshWebsite() call in save() re-fetches /website-settings which returns website_settings.store_name (stale override) instead of app_settings.store_name (newly saved). Before save: 'Rookie', New name: 'BugFixTest 4935', After ONE save: 'Test Partner Corp' (reverted to website_settings.store_name via refreshWebsite()).

TopNav shows 'Rookie' (stale website_settings.store_name) on initial load even though app_settings.store_name = 'PlatformEdit Test 5218'. This is a persistent inconsistency — not just after save. The website_settings collection has a stale store_name field that always overrides app_settings.store_name in the /website-settings merge endpoint.

integration_issues.issue: refreshWebsite() in WebsiteTab.tsx save() defeats the updateWebsite() call. After save(): (1) updateWebsite({store_name: 'BugFixTest 4935'}) updates context immediately, (2) refreshWebsite() fetches /website-settings which returns 'Test Partner Corp' from website_settings.store_name override, reverting the TopNav. Result: TopNav never shows the newly saved value.

action_items: Fix Bug 2: The /website-settings endpoint merge (website.py) overrides app_settings.store_name with a stale website_settings.store_name ('Rookie' for test-partner-corp). Fix options: (A) QUICKEST: In website.py GET /website-settings, exclude 'store_name' from the website_settings override dict so app_settings.store_name is always the authoritative source. Change line: **{k: v for k, v in web_s.items() if v is not None and k not in ('_id', 'tenant_id')} to **{k: v for k, v in web_s.items() if v is not None and k not in ('_id', 'tenant_id', 'store_name')}. (B) ALTERNATIVE: In PUT /admin/settings backend handler, also upsert website_settings.store_name = new_value so both collections stay in sync. (C) ALTERNATIVE FRONTEND: Remove refreshWebsite() from save() in WebsiteTab.tsx since updateWebsite() already provides correct immediate context update and refreshWebsite() is reverting to stale backend value.

Also consider: when platform admin updates tenant name via PUT /api/admin/tenants/{id}, the backend fix only updates app_settings.store_name but NOT website_settings.store_name. So even after Bug 1 is 'fixed', the TopNav still shows stale website_settings.store_name.
critical_code_review_comments: Root cause: website_settings collection contains a stale 'store_name' field from an older code version that wrote store_name to both app_settings AND website_settings. Current code only writes to app_settings via PUT /admin/settings, but the GET /website-settings merge still uses website_settings.store_name as an override (line ~317 in website.py). This causes persistent inconsistency between TopNav (from website-settings) and Organization Info input (from admin/settings).

refreshWebsite() in WebsiteTab.tsx save() is counterproductive - it fetches back the stale website_settings.store_name and reverts the updateWebsite() local state update. Either remove it or fix the backend merge to not let website_settings.store_name override app_settings.store_name.
The edit dialog title in TenantsTab shows tenant.name (e.g., 'StatusTest Name 4290') while the card displays store_name (e.g., 'BugFixTest 4935') — minor inconsistency but not breaking.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests renamed test-partner-corp tenant multiple times. Final tenant.name = 'PlatformEdit Test 5218', app_settings.store_name = 'PlatformEdit Test 5218'. website_settings.store_name = 'Rookie' (stale, never changed by tests).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug 1 (platform admin tenant name syncs app_settings.store_name) is FIXED and verified both backend and frontend. Bug 2 (partner admin single save updates TopNav) is NOT FIXED - the root cause is stale website_settings.store_name ('Rookie') overriding app_settings.store_name in /website-settings endpoint. Current test-partner-corp state: tenant.name='PlatformEdit Test 5218', app_settings.store_name='PlatformEdit Test 5218', website_settings.store_name='Rookie' (stale override). Credentials: platform admin=admin@automateaccounts.local/ChangeMe123! (code: automate-accounts), partner admin=alice@testpartner.com/TestPass123! (code: test-partner-corp). Key data-testids: [data-testid='ws-store-name'] (store name input), [data-testid='org-info-save-btn'] (save button), [data-testid='nav-logo'] (TopNav store name).

rca_of_issue.bug: Bug 2 - TopNav not updating after single save
rca_of_issue.root_cause: website_settings collection for test-partner-corp has a stale 'store_name' field set to 'Rookie' (from old code that wrote to both app_settings AND website_settings). The GET /website-settings merge endpoint at line ~317 in website.py applies website_settings fields as overrides AFTER setting store_name from app_settings. Since website_settings.store_name='Rookie' is not None, it always overrides app_settings.store_name. The refreshWebsite() call in save() fetches this merged result and sets context.store_name='Rookie', reverting the updateWebsite({store_name: newValue}) call made just before it.
reproduction_steps: 3. Note TopNav shows 'Rookie' while Store Name input shows 'PlatformEdit Test 5218'
4. Change Store Name to 'TestValue123' and click Save Changes ONCE
5. Observe: Toast shows 'Settings saved' but TopNav shows 'Test Partner Corp' (not 'TestValue123')
6. The TopNav briefly shows 'TestValue123' (from updateWebsite) then reverts to 'Test Partner Corp' (from refreshWebsite fetching stale website_settings.store_name)
rca_of_issue.mitigation: In website.py GET /website-settings endpoint, exclude 'store_name' from website_settings override dict so app_settings.store_name is always used. Or remove refreshWebsite() from WebsiteTab.tsx save() function.
